reviews.review_text:
Started this game thinking:
“hehe funny floating trash simulator”

Ended this game screaming at the ocean because of a shark named Bruce.

Raft is basically:

40% collecting garbage
30% dehydration
20% architectural engineering
10% pure hatred for Bruce

Bruce is not a shark.
Bruce is an unpaid employee whose full-time job is eating exactly the raft piece you JUST repaired.

“Oh nice, my raft is finally looki—”
CRUNCH
Thanks Bruce. Very cool.

Played on EASY mode and honestly? Zero regrets.
Some people want Dark Souls on water.
I wanted a chill survival game where I can build a floating Costco without developing real-life anxiety.

And the building system? Surprisingly addictive.
I started with:
“just a small raft”

A few hours later:
“welcome aboard my 3-story floating suburban shopping mall with advanced storage logistics.”

The story was actually way better than I expected too. The locations are cool, progression feels rewarding, and discovering new places kept me hooked.

BUT.

Finding Titanium feels like applying for a mortgage.
I swear the game acts like Titanium is made from extinct dinosaur tears.

After finishing the story, I really wish farming late-game materials became easier, because the building part is where this game becomes dangerously fun.

Honestly, I can totally see myself coming back just to play Creative Mode and build the dumbest floating civilization imaginable.

10/10
Would get emotionally bullied by a shark again.

As a 50 year old parent playing this with a 17 year old, I can highly recommend for most any age (may be a little too complex for under 8's) there is also a little blood spatter when the shark bites you but no real gore.  If you are a fan of Minecraft (or just exploration and crafting in general) this ticks all the boxes! Your raft is your home, build it in your own style! Fish, cook, expand your raft, collect supplies, explore islands, fight boars and bears for meat and leather, and search for clues needed to progress the game. Simplistic style, engaging, will keep you busy for quite a while and the co-op feature makes it even better! I haven't encountered any major bugs (I'm on a mid level gaming pc) I honestly love this game and have started my own world to solo after playing with my son. It doesn't disappoint!

Raft starts with 2x2
Shark eats 1 piece, 3 left
Repairs raft and expands
Learns shark's name is Bruce
Starts hating Bruce
Reaches end game
24 shark heads in container

10/10 would kill bruce again

Raft is basically “survival: ocean edition,” except the ocean has a personal vendetta against you. You start out peacefully floating on your tiny IKEA-quality plank of wood, thinking life is chill, and then the shark shows up like an unpaid intern who refuses to stop causing problems.

And honestly, the shark is the real main character. Not you. Not your survival story. Him. He’s just there 24/7, nibbling your raft like it owes him money, taking out foundation pieces with the confidence of someone who knows you can’t do anything about it right now. You can craft spears, build defenses, develop an entire floating empire… and he will still swing by just to ruin your day out of spite.

10/10 survival game. 0/10 emotional support from marine life.

It's a great game, you can finish it in a single dedicated Sunday. Gathering resources, exploring islands, and the co-op mode make it especially fun to play with friends. I highly recommend giving it a try.

It's good but I would've liked more rafting in this raft game.

The game starts out pretty rough, you're in a constant struggle to keep your bars full, crops and raft protected and gathering resources to actually progress in the game. It's a frustrating part of the game because there's no down time and progress is slow.
Once you get over that hump, you reinforce the raft and start to get some down time and agency the game has a great upswing. Building your ship at your leisure and exploring some islands.
*But* This is the point where the game decides the raft is no longer the focal point of the game. It's just the place you stand for 10 mins as you travel between story islands. Story islands you slowly have to walk around, doing simple puzzles and jabbing animals with a spear. So, pretty dull and not exactly leaning into the whole 'raft' gimmick.

Usually a game, advances by giving new challenges to overcome as you progress, no. The most challenging and engaging part of this game is towards the start, and then it just drifts to sleep leaving you having prepared for a challenge that never comes.

6.5/10

TLDR: Fun game if you don't expect much and you have company to play with it. Bad game if you look too hard at it. I looked too hard at it, so I didn't like it and I’m mostly just listing all the major reasons I could think of.


I'll be honest, I played it because I saw Markiplier's series a few years back. And honestly? As singleplayer, I liked the game until I didn't. I love survival games and I loved the survival aspect of the game. I would have recommended it if you asked me in the first 5-10 hours of playing,

But, as people have mentioned, it's very buggy. Most of the time, it's fine and you can ignore the bugs since they're kinda harmless. But, if you do any fighting or do the story quests, it gets incredibly frustrating and the inventory lost is very punishing (losing "2/3 of your inventory" when it's actually more like everything and your morgage). Hit boxes are weird and enemies tend to hit you way more than they can hit you. Like, hitting you through walls while you can't kind of janky. So, you just end up avoiding fights entirely. 

Quick mentions, the game can be lackluster if you look at the seams too hard. The islands you find around are very repetitive and predictable after the first 2-3 encounters, having the same rewards n the same places. And I found myself ending up with a bunch of things in surplus you quickly have no purpose for that you either must throw away (fearing they will become relevant in the future again) or increase your raft size and then add chests for just for them. The eating and drinking mechanics are annoying if you don't always carry a lot of water and food with you since your character has the faster metabolism known to mankind. The nighttime sucks always and the story feels very chaotic (maybe because they released the chapters at different times and experimented with stuff?). 

Speaking of the story (no spoilers), it was original and it had good puzzles, I'll give it that. But I realized I only enjoyed them when I already knew how they went from vague memories of Markiplier playing it. But, if you have no idea, I find that the puzzles are really convoluted and weird, which you'd think would add charm to the game with the cartoony aesthetic. Instead, it just makes you sit there confused and kind of annoyed because you spent thirty minutes to an hour going around the location looking for the one thing you need and you end up just looking up online. The chapters felt connected in the weirdest way, making it hard to care or simply get attached to it.
 
 I'll be real with you guys; at the end, there was no fun. I just wanted it to end. I wanted to be proven that the game did get better in the late game when I saw the quality being mediocre. It was just so much useless back and forth for the sake of making you waste time. They did parkour segments, but the character physics isn’t made for it, and the hitbox is still buggy. They mixed puzzles with fighting without any warning. They added weird temporary mechanics. There’s a lot of wasted potential and empty zones. All that for one of the most unsatisfying endings I've seen. Like, yeah, there's a cutscene. Yet I’ve had single screens of texts be so much more satisfying than that. 

Anyway, I say all this negative, but the core game is ok. If you want a cheap game to play with friends that has lots of shenanigan potential, I’d imagine this would be real fun. But, as someone who wanted to play a good survival game on my own, it wasn’t it.

I have on and off played this game a decent amount, solo and with friends and I just love it. Gameplay is so nice, the additions of animals, new destinations and items are great and I hope they keep coming.

10/10 game!!! I love this game so much! It's a great cozy game to play with friends, and I love all the crafting mechanics. My friends and I usually have little jobs we all pick up as we sail through the world, and it's so fun to have a coop game to work together and goof around in. The story is also super fun and interesting, which makes the game even more fun. I think we started playing the game before the story was really implemented, so it's been fantastic to watch this game grow and flourish as it has over the years. I 100% recommend this game :D

I have been telling my sister that we should get this game for a while and she never listened to me. Now that she has, we have been addicted, not even on the story aspect yet and we're hooked (get it). I found this game because of Markiplier and I knew I had to play it.
Very good game i want to flay olof alive, the Npc talking animation makes me giggle every time.

The story so far is so amazing, im currently going through it again for the third time! The puzzles in the game are so well thought out! my favourite is the shipping container puzzle in tangaroa. I hope they add more to the story, like how did the world flood? whats the next steps for utopia? etc. Also i think we should be able to attack the shark with an axe! defo a 10/10, would recommend
A very fun game that gives the option to return without losing anything when playing alone
its a cool game i fuess but are there any advice for completing chapter one?

Fun, not as terribly deep as it could be, but easy enough to get the basics of. Basically never run out of water and food and you can go at any pace afterwards. The shark is an ass, the bird is an ass, and I'd imagine this game is a lot more fun with friends. Couldn't tell you.

This game can be very fun and I have logged forty hours playing, but overall has MAJOR balancing issues with the enemies in my opinion. 
Before getting into that, let's talk about the positives.
-scratching the survival itch
If you're looking for sandbox survival games then look no further as this is one of them. Subnautica and the Forest are the most similar to this game in my opinion but enjoyers of other survival games will likely find joy and value in this game.
Progression is unbelievably slow. I have done 40 hours of gameplay and I am in chapter 2 of the story. I bead RDR2 in about 20. Subnatica took me 24, I usually pour 30hrs into Minecraft worlds before getting bored with them. 40 hours for only being in chapter 2 of the story is some Witcher-ass length of gameplay. The story itself is intriguing and engaging when it's there, but it is so sparse I am not sure it is enough to carry me through to the end of the game with how awful getting into death loops is.
To the balancing issue: I'm a reasonably seasoned gamer, I don't do 100% no damage runs but neither am I accustomed to dying all the time in games on normal difficulty. However, I notice with this game, one thing happens and then you get into these awful repeated loops where you are frantically trying to get back to your raft, trying to get items, trying to complete a last story thing, get a final object on an island, and you are just getting completely shredded by the mobs in this game. The bears respawn after 2 minutes. 2 MINUTES. 120 SECONDS. The squawker over Caravan Town drops a rock on your head EVERY THIRTY SECONDS. With the salve and armor given to you at that point in the game, it is literally impossible to not repeatedly die to the mobs. And don't even get me started on the f***ing shark. Sometimes it leaves you alone and sometimes it knows you've got barely any health and are running away from things and chomps on you.
Overall they combine into a very difficult and not at-all rewarding experience that requires you throw yourself at the wall until you are lucky enough to get through the next story phase without dying.
The shark sucks. He ate my boat and my ass. 10/10 just for the part were you run out of thirst after 5 hours of grinding.

Very fun game! I enjoy having my base be a vehicle instead of the two being seperate! I would recommend this game to anyone who enjoys survival and base building, or people who like having a vehicle base that you can costumize.
2nd best survival game of all time.Minecraft is more complete,however if you look for something new dont look further that raft.

This is one of those cases where I wish Steam had a "Don't really swing either way" review, but in the lack of this, I'll swing towards "Don't recommend".

The gameplay loop is great, and has a lot of interesting ideas. Expanding the raft, making contraptions to make the raft livable and interesting to sustain oneself on, and making sure you don't get harrassed by the shark by reinforcing it wherever necessary, then getting into different islands to get into the story.

The problem comes with a thorough lack of proper conveyance on a few things, like some items do not tell you "how to get them" (like how to get seaweed for the glue), as well as some things being not only tedious, like storage and overall building (why aren't there signs that can just be nailed straight to the thing rather than dangling on a rope and taking so much unnecessary space?!), or cultivating trees/bushes being thoroughly unrewarding due to not having any ways to sustain seeds.

It also doesn't help that there are some really questionable design decisions, like the shark respawning mere minutes having being killed, which is nice when food is an issue and lets you have ways to feed yourself, but then also drops trophies, which clutters the inventory REAL fast, on top of making underwater exploration for important scavenging an actual misery. Making shark bait doesn't help either, and nets you roughly the same amount of time.

And add on top of that the hilariously bad glitches (Bow is actually a gatling gun in disguise, that doesn't need full charge to deal max damage and can be shot underwater, oneshotting sharks), and the terrible optimization (this game is literally just sea and random trash, why is my 3080Ti fully maxed out at low-mid settings?!) and you get a very rough experience on what should, by all means, be a wonderful game.

So yeah, this game does a lot of very unique things, that I really like. It also has a lot of things that should be fixed (like the performance) and needs some better QoL options (like customization of the shark timers, tool degradation rates and seed rates for trees, trophy drop rates or a dedicated unlimited trophy dump) to truly shine. As it is though, it's really not something I'd recommend fully. It's unique, but not enough to truly warrant a full recommendation.

Tips for raft players. 
1. There are different modes for different play styles - choose carefully. 
2. No tutorial, so I always recommend putting an hour or so into peaceful mode just to get the hang of things. 
3. The Q button. If this bothers you change it in the settings. I made it the Y and that was a lot more helpful. 
4. Co-op can be fun with the right people. 
5. The beginning is a grind. You can plant, eat, and cook potatos and beets. (This took me wayyy to long to figure out) 

I thought that might be a good way for those looking at playing this game to get a hint into what they are getting into. This game can be great but is not perfect and is a bit older. Turning up the graphics makes a massive difference. 

Stab Bruce, then mount his head. Stab him again but leave his head. He will not spawn for a while so you can breathe. Bruce is the shark. 

I played with my husband and we had so much fun.

Love, love, love the game. Hope it will keep expending and get updates. I am ready to go further and discover many new things and places. Thank you.

Very fun relaxing game. Just hard enough to be challenging at times. I agree, Late game farming is pretty ridiculous but if your like me, you would complete everything before you officially beat the game. Late game farming isnt "needed" but makes everything on crack, which is why i assume its so hard to farm

Played the entire game with 5 other friends, and only the first 5 hours of it and the animals was good, but everything else was badly paced and poor storytelling, during and after the the forest island with the bears, the rest of the islands proved way too annoying of fetch quest and puzzles that requires a wide spanned team to get it done faster. 
Not to mention the crafting and recipes, we get like high tier stuff even though its like at the end of the game which served no more purpose because we already have all that we need and prepare. 
Overall a 1/10
very nice love this game. i have a potato for a pc and everything still looks great! the story islands are a little ruff but thats just because of the detail! love this game

Very fun friendslop . can rack up many fun hours on the seas adventuring with friends and building your raft but would not recommend for people who are going to play the game solo. I played the whole story with one other person and dont think it would have been nearly as good if i was by myself.

absolute raft, loved playing this game with friend hunting achievements was great (we don't speak of expert screecher hunter)

Played this right after beating Subnautica. It's hard to decide which is my favourite game of all time. I'm thinking Subnautica, but that's most-likely because I'm currently playing Subnautica 2. If Red Beet released a Raft 2, I'm sure I'd be leaning towards Raft as my GOAT. It's one of the most compelling and immersive experiences you can ever play, with very simple, yet hardcore mechanics. The sense of freedom, exploration, and agency are unmatched in most games I've ever played. If you haven't played this, then you should give it a shot.
I bought it when it came out, it's a everything I love in a game. Very peaceful and fun. I highly recommend this game.

super fun and chill game.
solo or with friends is a great experience, you really feel like your working towards something.
upgrading tools and getting better materials aswell as upgrading equipment/machines is great.
story is fine, but thats not what you play this game for. 8.5/10

A truly miserable experience.

The "open world survival craft" genre has become my favourite type of game, and Raft was on my radar for a long time. It was well-reviewed and I was saving it for a rainy day based on the assumption that it would be good. Unfortunately, it's not good.

Everything in the game seems unfinished. The movement is imprecise and janky. So is the combat, which the game expects you to participate in. None of it feels pleasant. Everything you do feels about 30% too slow -- from the walking and sprinting speeds, to the time between tool swings, to the time it takes between eating a food item and that item disappearing from your inventory and allowing a new item to be picked up in the slot it previously occupied. All of it will make you feel like you're playing through brain damage.

The crafting/harvesting/survival mechnics are not tuned well. You'll be running in repetitive ellipses on your raft attending to your tedious survival chores without a moment to breathe and take in the vibes. Your food and water meters deplete too quickly, and nutritional items replenish you too little.

The exploration of islands is very disappointing, and they're homogeneous slop piles full of graphical assets that look like you should be able to harvest or interact with them, but which you cannot, full of hostile creatures that are annoying to fight. Islands seem to spawn from a small roster of pre-gens, but they're universally boring.  The bespoke islands that are part of the story aren't much better, 

The shark who follows you all game is one of the most annoying mechanics ever devised. It will bite your raft every 5 minutes, and you either ward it off or let it destroy one (1) tile of raft. Either way you pay a slow, constant material tax for repairing your raft's shark-inflicted wounds. There's nothing interesting about interacting with the shark directly, whether you're trying to kill it, avoid it, or escape it, because your interaction is mediated through the game's sloppy, slow movement and combat systems. There aren't really any interesting items or equipment to alter the sharks behaviour (other than a bait item to distract it). The shark is on a hard respawn timer, so if you want to drop everything you're doing to kill it, you will earn yourself 3 minutes of peace. It could've been such an interesting nemesis system, that changes as the story advances and you can craft more complex gear, but instead your sharky foe is insanely undercooked like the rest of the game.

In terms of good things about Raft... well, as far as I'm aware, it was the first survival crafter to make your base a mobile vehicle, and that's a good gimmick that other games have since copied. Props for the idea. The music is also good, and there's at least one track that's an all-time jaunty wistful adventurin' banger.

I suspect the high review average for Raft is compromised by all the co-op players. A robust co-op mode can quite literally turn a bad mechanic into a fun time, as you groan and gripe about it with your buds. I can see the co-op experience being preferable to solo, but I can also see co-op becoming boring a lot quicker, once surviving stops being difficult.

Raft is like a C-tier or D-tier entry in the genre, and not worth playing until you've exhausted all the better games that are its peers. Even after that,
Great game, easy concept, superb execution

super fun game. and WAY better if you have atleast one friend. ive got a raft ive been doing with my friend and we've made crazy progress. researched a lot so far but not gotten to any story parts of the game lol.

One of the best games to play with the family. The game takes a while to finish so you never get bored, IT IS WORTH 19.99$. The graphics are good, but the story IS ONE OF THE BEST STORIES I HAVE EVER PLAYED IN GAME. In conclusion if you think about buying this, I would recommend it has a good plot, Its cheap for what it gives you, and its pretty family friendly.
so you got a raft right and theres a really mean shark. game becomes good for the 2 minutes or so that the shark doesnt respawn after killing it
Fun. Don't play it alone! It's much more fun with friends. But if you're ever lonely, you always have your shark friend. :)
i got like 160 hours or sm in this game, very very peak game. played alone and with friends, all fantastic memories
I really love the game its pretty fun and i would recommend others to play this game i wish they would add more to the game but I am guessing they wont. A DLC would be cool tho

Raft is one of those games that you have to play AT LEAST ONCE with each friend group. A true friend group co-op from before the era of friendslop. It was a blast to play as new content was released and worth at least one playthrough since its been finished. 

Highly recommended.

so the game is very good and optimized actually, and even a 1050 gpu can run it. very good graphics and advanced story line with the different islands u need to find like that city one and the others. such a good game get it.

My sons and I grinded out this game in 3 days on vacation on cheap laptops. We loved it! We really enjoy games like Terraria, Stardew valley and Valheim. Once we started playing Raft we couldn't stop. We each had our own roles to help one another progress through the game. From upgrading the raft, navigating and food management it was very fun. I only wish they would add more chapters.
Amazing game this might be one of the best if not the best survival game of all time, i have played this so many times with my friends and have had a ton of fun, trust and get the game.

I can recommend playing this, it's a fun 'open world' survival sandbox, but. BUT.

I have one HUGE caveat to add: the durability of your craftable equipment is abyssmal. Yes, durability improves with improved equipment versions, but even then ♥♥♥♥ still breaks WAY too quickly. And it's not as if you can recycle the broken equipment for some materials. Also, not all equipment has better versions. 
Some important equipment you'll find yourself just recrafting over and over.

Dealing with durability gives me more anxiety than I want to experience in a game I play for fun.

This also means that for exploring larger areas where will not have access to your base, you either fill about 2/3rds of your already limited inventory with extra equipment you expect to be using, or you'll be constantly backtracking to your base when a pivotal piece of equipment breaks.

This problem is further compounded when playing solo because there's no division of labor. For a large part of the game you are continuously occuppied with maintaining food/drink levels as well as producing materials and equipment, while maintaining your base.

I've been playing this game off and on for years during its development (all solo). I love everything about this game: the exploration, incrementally unlocking better technologies, the way the story is told, the music. Love it! But on default settings the durability issues have always made me break off a playthrough sooner or later.

I hate mods in general. They can be buggy, they can ruin your experience/break your immersion, your playthrough can run into problems when the game updates and an installed mods doesn't, they can contain viruses, so I almost never use them. But for my current playthrough I decided to install a mod that makes equipment have infinite durability. Subnautica-style. And I've been really enjoying myself for the first time I play this game. I really feel survival games like these should have more customisability built-in when it comes to the survivability aspects, things like item durability. These are such simple parameters to make available for the player, we really shouldn't need third-party programs for things like this.

The game were wonderful and I can express my creativity here, but I'm writing this review as lately I have experienced an issue launching the game after I updated my NVIDIA Graphic Card Driver to the latest version 610.47 that released on Tuesday May 26, 2026. 

Firstly, before updating to the latest version of GPU driver the game launch and works normally, but after updating to the latest version the are constantly crashed and not responded. I have reseached and tried many solution online without expecting the GPU driver would be the issue. 

Finally, after tried rolling back my NVIDIA Graphic Card Driver to the previous version 596.36 that released on Tuesday May 12, 2026 the issue was instantly fixed and the game can launch itself and works normally. 

Hope my review were acknowledge and taking onto consideration, also anyone who experienced the same problem for now can consider rolling back your GPU driver to the previous version if you wanted to play this game. #HopeThisHelping
well balanced and fun survival crafting game

Was on the fence about this game but after playing 14 hours so far it is worth the buy, game is at your own pace so no need to rush through, and hidden caves and so much more to explore and go through. So far so good, just get it you wont be disappointed if your looking for your next survival game.

10/10

I want more.
Game is too short, and one of the few games I've completed with my girlfriend.
We wish for more content or a new game.

10/10

10/10 worth the money somehow every difficulty is a different playstyle, easy is a chill survival game normal is normal im too scared to play hard and creative is like creative mode in minecrfat but with raft. I gotta actually fully complete the game one of these days i havent really ever gotten passed a upgraded water purifier cuz i die and lose everything
Worth playing this game. Although, I have a love and hate relationship with surprised attacks from the Screechers and Bruce, whenever I lose myself in the serenity of daily tasks.
Pretty fun but gets boring quick. And has almost no activity. So I would buy this on sale if I were you..

The game is split into 2 phases: The raft survival phase, and the story exploration phase. 

The raft survival phase is super fun, and is worth the money alone. 10/10, you can usually get 10-30 hours out of it, and it is very replayable. If you don't get motion sick from the waves, you'll love this survival game. 

I personally hate linier games, so I sadly hate the story exploration phase too. I really wanted to like it since I liked the first half of the game so much, but I could never find the will to keep playing despite how many times I tried with different qol mods, friends, or updates. It was just boring and unrewarding for me, and when I finally watched a playthrough I saw there wasn't anything more. If raft was just it's story, I'd refund it. Raft really should've been 2 separate games.
Great game. Can be pretty relaxing when you're not fighting something.

While I can't speak for the singleplayer experience, I can definitely say that I've really enjoyed playing Raft with some of my friends. Very well executed, and has just the perfect amount of bugs to make gameplay... "interesting"!

Runs a solid 60fps at max graphics for me.
if you play this game often, play it with friends as it is so much funnier and better
Raft is a very fun game with Ali and ♥♥♥ GUZZLER because they know what to do and they are my only friends/

Raft may disguise itself as a game about exploring, building and surviving on a ramshackle boat in a post-apocalyptic world, but frankly, it is actually all about cleaning the ocean. Thanks to me, the Ocean Cleanup® has filed for bankruptcy.
good game little toothy guy kinda scary and ocean kinda scary but overal like an 8/10. :)

Cool game, but seems to be lacking substance.  Easily explored every area, with the exception past the Leviathan, seems unlikely to go past him.  It's way to scary.  This being a survival game I don't want to ever die.  I take surviving extremely serious.  I play other games that have perma death and when that happens the game ends for me. Water usage is way to fast.  Seems like the original Subnautica had way more stuff to make.  The game runs great on my PC even when I am running all 4 of my screens at the same time.  I love the polished look and I didn't use any spoilers. The detail is extremely amazing.  The larger fish in the heated areas are pretty OP without a way to hurt them.  It would be cool to have a spear gun where you could deliver wounds and creatures would run off, but make it difficult to hit them.  Also the world would need to be much larger when the game is complete.  Maybe further missions that require finding rare artifacts and way to construct a ship to leave the planet in the final end game.  Furthermore add other survivors that you could trade with.  just some suggestions of what I am into.  This might not even be vision of the game.  Art and fluidness is really really good.  The game is very smooth.  I would recommend it to others.  Look forward to seeing further updates.

From Homeless on a 2x2 Square to an International Waste-Management TycoonI bought this game thinking it would be a cinematic, relaxing voyage across a gorgeous, endless blue ocean. I imagined myself strumming a guitar under the starlight while gently drifting past tropical islands. Instead, Raft turned me into a frantic, hyperventilating garbage collector who spends 90% of their day playing tug-of-war with a hyper-aggressive shark over a single piece of floating plastic. It is the only survival game that forces you to realize the ocean isn't a majestic natural wonder; it’s just a giant, wet highway full of free construction materials and unmitigated stress.The Traumatic Early Game (Plastic Hook Simulator)You wake up stranded on a pathetic four-tile wooden platform with absolutely nothing to your name except a plastic hook on a rope. Your survival instinct instantly takes over, and you spend your first three hours wildly throwing this hook into the water like an unhinged fisherman, desperately reeling in floating leaves, barrels, and literal trash just to survive. Your throat is drier than a desert, your stomach is screaming, and you are frantically trying to build a primitive cup to purify exactly two sips of ocean water before you keel over. The pacing is intense: pull garbage, boil water, plant a potato, try not to die.The Ultimate Unpaid Employee: Bruce the SharkYou cannot talk about Raft without talking about the real main character of the game: Bruce the Shark. Bruce is not just a predator; he is a dedicated saboteur whose full-time job is to wait until you have finally gathered enough resources to expand your base, only to swim up and aggressively bite off the exact tile you just upgraded. You will find yourself crafted a wooden spear, standing on the edge of your raft, and screaming obscenities into your microphone at a digital animal at 2:00 AM because he ate your favorite storage chest. Eventually, you stop fearing Bruce and start treating him like a highly annoying, toothy landlord who demands a tax payment of one foundation tile every ten minutes.  The Multi-Story Luxury Cruise Ship EscalationThe progression in this game makes absolutely zero sense in the best way possible. You start out struggling to stay afloat on a few planks of wood, but by hour forty, you have single-handedly engineered a towering, three-story floating metropolis. Your humble raft now features an advanced steering wheel, multiple loud engine rooms, automated collection nets, a fully functional kitchen, a goat pasture on the second floor, and a rooftop zipline system. You aren't a shipwreck survivor anymore; you are a high-tech naval architect piloting a massive recycling plant across the globe, completely decim Jane’s local ecosystem every time you anchor near a tiny reef to strip-mine it for iron ore.Co-op Mode: Architectural DictatorshipPlaying this game with friends completely alters the psychological dynamic of the survival experience. You will inevitably divide into two distinct roles. Player One becomes the sweat-lord navigator, spending hours looking at charts, managing the sails, and organizing thirty different chests labeled "Planks Only." Player Two, meanwhile, will completely ignore the survival mechanics, spend all of the team's hard-earned copper ingots to craft a decorative rug, jump off the boat without a weapon, and immediately get mauled by a screeching eagle on a nearby island, forcing you to stop the engines and launch a rescue mission.Final Verdict: 10/10. It is a brilliant, incredibly satisfying crafting loop disguised as a maritime disaster. It turns the chore of picking up litter into a high-stakes adventure. Buy it immediately, craft a metal spear, and show Bruce who truly owns the open sea.If this review helped you successfully reel in a floating barrel from maximum distance, throw it a thumbs up so I can buy more plastic to patch up my kitchen floor.
Fun, challenging, dangerous. Its like subnautica but more time on the surface than underwater. Still have to go underwater :D

holy friendslop but high quality and fun. finished it like 20 times already its pretty cool
pretty fun game once u get into it. alot of ♥♥♥♥♥♥ grinding in the start, but once the game opens up and u get to tier2-tier3 and can chill more the game is very fun.  7/10

Raft is a really fun survival game that is simple, easy to understand, and just fun to play. I liked how much creativity there is with building your raft and slowly turning it into whatever you want. The game has cool locations, fun puzzles, and enough stuff to do that it never really gets boring. I enjoyed it so much that I ended up completing all 104 achievements, so I would definitely recommend it.

great game! a lot better to play with friends rather than solo, wish there were still updates but the story is engaging :) after finishing storymode theres still stuff to do which is neat, but afterwards it kinda dies down. theres some replay value, but i dont see myself playing it more than twice (storymode wise).
Love this game. Got it to play with my family initially, but soon started playing it in my own time. So much fun. But tricky at first to grasp how the game works, however.

Bottom line is, this game is made very well.
It's fun solo, but a lot of fun with friends... I think it's worth every penny the only thing would be to add a bit more customization maybe, or event sharks but that's just for spice.

this game is awesome with friends i got a gpu for 30 bucks and it runs it in great graphics its fun building game you can explore islands and farm its awesome its worth my money if your looking for an expirience with friends this is your pick

One of the best story games had to do it twice. Very nice progression and long interesting plot. 10/10

good survival game like the open sea bc i dont fear it. Good story overall perfect that there is no pay to win

One of the more fun Crafting/Survival games to play with friends. Constantly collecting and building the raft, fending off the shark, traveling from island to island it almost makes you feel like a member of the Straw Hat pirates from One Piece. Most Crafting/Survival games get pretty stale to me but Raft keeps you moving constantly, its extremely dynamic in that you have multiple things to do at once and the discoveries make you really feel like exploration is worth it, especially with the lore of the game being as subtle as it is. Definitely try this if you're into games like Minecraft, The Forest and the like.

I've enjoyed every second of playing this game. The storyline is captivating and hooked me pretty early. Even after finishing the game, I would play expanding my raft and collecting excess resources. For its price this game is a gem. I'd give it a solid 9/10.

Happy to finally actually play this after years of watching others play it. Honestly loved this game with friends and I definitely couldn’t do a lot without them. 9/10, giving it a 9 instead of a full 10 because it does sometimes get buggy.

Great multiplayer survival-builder game.  I loved the hook, the fishing, the tech system, and the game can feel very rewarding.  Solo however the shark gets annoying, and the game overall lacks a variety of assets/enemies.  Raft is a simplistic game and there isn't much to it, but what it does do, it does well.
4/5

The game for me is one of those games where you have so much fun with your friends just from the pure silliness of the game. From the silly animations and the movement techs you have whether through glitches or other bugs there are, to naming the shark and getting frustrated but the pure joy and excitement you get from its death. 

It really does make you think, especially when dealing with Bob. It is one of those games that is not the most intuitive, but some of the quality of life stuff like crafting other materials when trying to craft the large things you need, to laughing at how someone eats a watermelon.

We haven't gotten far in the story mode, if anywhere at all, but I'm excited to keep playing this world. I enjoy that all the worlds feel different, and that there are so many different circumstances that you can encounter. We strategized as we fought the Screecher and it worked. While the graphics can be kind of intensive, the game is overall serene outside of the random Bob (the shark we named) heart attack.
